prompt: A small widget factory operates for 8 hours a day, producing widgets at a constant rate. On Monday, they started at 9:00 AM. For the first 3 hours, their production rate was 25 widgets per hour. From 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM, a critical machine experienced a partial malfunction, reducing the overall production rate by 20%. After 2:00 PM, the machine was fully repaired, and the remaining work hours until the end of the 8-hour shift were used to produce widgets at an increased rate of 35 widgets per hour to catch up. At the end of the day, a quality control check revealed that 10% of all widgets produced were defective. How many *non-defective* widgets were produced on Monday?
